stories_covered: Scale AI acquired by Meta for $14.8B with special dividend
Discord CEO Jason Citron replacement speculation
harry_takes: Meta's $14.8B Scale deal is really about buying relevance in AI, not financial returns; founder leaving signals dead company
Microsoft has no leverage in OpenAI relationship because AGI is coming soon regardless
The old guard cannot compete with new AI-native companies despite technical resources
Founder-led companies should stay the course; board-forced CEO changes destroy value most of the time
harry_boldest_quote: I think it was an opportunity. I tell all founders to sell now, by the way.
predictions: Garrett (Handshake): Scale will exceed $100M revenue within one year, demand tripled immediately
Lemkin: Scale revenue will decline significantly due to customer reallocation to competing data providers
Harry: Apple will announce US iPhone assembly operations this year
Jason: Chinese AI model will reach #1 ranking in benchmarks this year
disagreements: Harry disagrees with Microsoft having leverage vs OpenAI, while another panelist (likely Lemkin) argues Microsoft has substantial leverage. Rory takes the pessimistic bet on S&P 500 finishing negative at 70% odds, while Jason remains bullish. On old-guard tech competitiveness, Harry expresses lack of confidence while Rory argues the issue is situational positioning, not inherent capability.
harry_role: Harry moderates the discussion, asks probing strategic questions about deal valuations and founder dynamics, and shares his own skeptical takes on Meta's motivations and old-guard AI competitiveness. He challenges panelists while maintaining authority as host, and frequently seeks to reframe questions when he disagrees with framing.
notable: Garrett from Handshake reveals demand tripled in one week post-Scale acquisition, and the unusual deal structure leaves Scale as a declining-revenue business despite $14.8B nominal valuation.
